title: NEBNext Poly(A) mRNA Isolation and RNA Library Prep Protocol
author: New England Biolabs
materials: NEBNext Poly(A) mRNA Magnetic Isolation Module
NEBNext Ultra II Directional RNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina

To wash the Oligo dT Beads, add the following to a 1.5 ml nuclease-free tube. If preparing multiple libraries, beads for up to 10 samples can be added to a single 1.5 ml tube for subsequent washes (use magnet NEB #S1506 for 1.5 ml tubes). The purpose of this step is to bring the beads from the storage buffer into the binding buffer. The 2X Binding Buffer does not have to be diluted for this step. %s %s
Proceed directly to Second Strand cDNA Synthesis.

[ ! ] Dilute the NEBNext Adaptor* prior to setting up the ligation reaction in ice-cold Adaptor Dilution Buffer and keep the adaptor on ice. %s %s Total RNA Input Dilution Required 1,000 ng–101 ng 5–fold dilution in Adaptor Dilution Buffer 100 ng–10 ng 25–fold dilution in Adaptor Dilution Buffer 5 ng 200–fold dilution in Adaptor Dilution Buffer *The NEBNext adaptor is provided in NEBNext oligos kit. NEB has several oligo kit options, which are supplied separately from the library prep kit. Please see www.neb.com/oligos for additional information.
Proceed immediately to Purification of the Ligation Reaction.

steps.title: Prepare the First Strand Synthesis Reaction...
steps.instruction: Prepare the First Strand Synthesis Reaction Buffer and Random Primer Mix in a nuclease-free microcentrifuge tube as follows: %s %s You can prepare the first strand synthesis reaction buffer later in the protocol, but it is important that it is ready before the elution in step 38. The beads should not be allowed to dry out.

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times. Keep the mix on ice until mRNA is purified. It will be used in Step 38 and 41.

steps.title: Dilute the total RNA with nuclease-free...
steps.instruction: Dilute the total RNA with nuclease-free water to a final volume of 50 µL in a nuclease-free 0.2 ml PCR tube and keep on ice.

steps.title: Wash the beads by pipetting up and down 6 times
steps.instruction: Wash the beads by pipetting up and down 6 times.

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnet and incubate at room temperature until the solution is clear (~ 00:02:00 ).

steps.title: Remove and discard all of the supernatant from the tube
steps.instruction: Remove and discard all of the supernatant from the tube. Take care not to disturb the beads.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack.

steps.title: Add 100 µL RNA Binding Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL RNA Binding Buffer (2X) to the beads and wash by pipetting up and down six times. If preparing multiple libraries, add 100 µL RNA Binding Buffer (2X) per sample. The Binding Buffer does not have to be diluted.

steps.title: Place the tubes on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on the magnet and incubate at room temperature until the solution is clear (~ 00:02:00 ).

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant from the tube
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant from the tube. Take care not to disturb the beads.

steps.title: Add 50 µL RNA Binding Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL RNA Binding Buffer (2X) to the beads and mix by pipetting up and down until beads are homogenous. If preparing multiple libraries, add 50 µL RNA Binding Buffer (2X) per sample. This first binding step removes most of the non target RNA.

steps.title: Add 50 μl beads to each...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μl beads to each RNA sample from Step 3. Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 6 times.

steps.title: Place the tube in a thermocycler and close the lid
steps.instruction: Place the tube in a thermocycler and close the lid. Heat the sample at 65 °C for 00:05:00 and cool to 4 °C with the heated lid set at ≥ 75 °C to denature the RNA and facilitate binding of the mRNA to the beads.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the thermocycler when the temperature reaches 4 °C .

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 6 times
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 6 times. Place the tube on the bench and incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 to allow the mRNA to bind to the beads.

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnetic rack at room temperature until the solution is clear (~ 00:02:00 ).

steps.title: Remove and discard all of the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard all of the supernatant. Take care not to disturb the beads.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack.

steps.title: Wash the beads by adding 200...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads by adding 200 µL of Wash Buffer to the tube to remove unbound RNA. Gently pipette the entire volume up and down 6 times to mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnetic rack at room temperature until the solution is clear (~ 00:02:00 ).

steps.title: Remove and discard all of the supernatant from the tube
steps.instruction: Remove and discard all of the supernatant from the tube. Take care not to disturb the beads.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack.

steps.title: Repeat steps 20–23
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 20–23. go to step #20

steps.title: Add 50 μl of Tris Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μl of Tris Buffer (provided in NEB #E7490 kit) to each tube. Gently pipette up and down 6 times to mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Place the tube on the thermocycler
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the thermocycler. Close the lid and heat the samples at 80 °C for 00:02:00 , then cool to 25 °C with the heated lid set at ≥ 90 °C to do the first elution of the mRNA from the beads.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the thermocycler when the temperature reaches 25 °C .

steps.title: Add 50 μl of RNA Binding...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μl of RNA Binding Buffer (2X) to the sample to allow the mRNA to re-bind to the beads. Mix thoroughly by gently pipetting up and down six times.

steps.title: Incubate the tube at room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the tube at room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnetic rack at room temperature until the solution is clear (~ 00:02:00 ).

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant from the tube
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant from the tube. Take care not to disturb the beads.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack.

steps.title: Wash the beads by adding 200 µL of Wash Buffer
steps.instruction: Wash the beads by adding 200 µL of Wash Buffer. Gently pipette the entire volume up and down 6 times to mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Spin down the tube briefly to...
steps.instruction: Spin down the tube briefly to collect the liquid from the wall and lid of the tube. It is important to spin down the tube to prevent carryover of the Wash Buffer in subsequent steps.

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnet at room temperature until the solution is clear (~ 00:02:00 ).

steps.title: Remove and discard all of the supernatant from the tube
steps.instruction: Remove and discard all of the supernatant from the tube. Take care not to disturb the beads that contains the mRNA. It is important to remove all of the supernatant to successfully fragment the mRNA in the subsequent steps. Spin down the tube. Place the tube on the magnetic rack and with a 10 μl tip, remove all of the wash buffer. (Caution: Do not disturb beads that contain the mRNA). Avoid letting the beads dry out before adding elution buffer.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack. [ ! ] The next step provides a fragmentation incubation time resulting in an RNA insert size of ~ 200 nt. For RNA insert sizes > 200 nt, refer to Appendix A in the manual for recommended fragmentation times in Step 39.

steps.title: To elute the mRNA from the...
steps.instruction: To elute the mRNA from the beads and fragment, add 11.5 µL of the First Strand Synthesis Reaction Buffer and Random Primer Mix (2X) prepared in Step 2, pipette up and down six times to resuspend the beads.

steps.title: Incubate the sample in a preheated...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample in a preheated thermocycler with the heated lid set at 105 °C as follows: 00:15:00 at 94 °C Hold at 4 °C * *Immediately transfer the tube to ice for 00:01:00 as soon as it is cool enough to handle (~ 65 °C )

steps.title: Quickly spin down the tube in...
steps.instruction: Quickly spin down the tube in a microcentrifuge to collect the liquid from the sides of the tube and place on the magnet right away until the solution is clear (~1–2 minutes).

steps.title: Collect the fragmented mRNA by transferring...
steps.instruction: Collect the fragmented mRNA by transferring 10 µL of the supernatant to a nuclease-free 0.2 ml PCR tube. If the supernatant volume recovered is less than 10 μl for any reason, bring the volume up to 10 μl by adding the First Strand Synthesis Reaction Buffer and Random Primer Mix prepared in Step 2 and continue with the protocol. Avoid transferring any of the magnetic beads.

steps.title: Place the tube on ice and...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on ice and proceed directly to First Strand cDNA Synthesis.

steps.title: A B 1 First Strand Synthesis...
steps.instruction: A B 1 First Strand Synthesis Reaction Volume 2 Fragmented and Primed RNA (

steps.title: 10 µl 3 NEBNext Strand Specificity...
steps.instruction: 10 µl 3 NEBNext Strand Specificity Reagent 8 µl 4 NEBNext First Strand Synthesis Enzyme Mix 2 µl 5 Total Volume 20 µl First Strand Synthesis Reaction Volume Fragmented and Primed RNA (

steps.title: 10 µl NEBNext Strand Specificity Reagent...
steps.instruction: 10 µl NEBNext Strand Specificity Reagent 8 µl NEBNext First Strand Synthesis Enzyme Mix 2 µl Total Volume 20 µl NEBNext Strand Specificity Reagent New England Biolabs Catalog #E7766 NEBNext First Strand Synthesis Enzyme Mix New England Biolabs Catalog #E7761

steps.title: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10 times.

steps.title: Assemble the second strand cDNA synthesis...
steps.instruction: Assemble the second strand cDNA synthesis reaction on ice by adding the following components into the first strand synthesis product from Step 45). A B 1 Second Strand Synthesis Reaction Volume 2 First Strand Synthesis Product (Step 45) 20 µl 3 NEBNext Second Strand Synthesis Reaction Buffer with dUTP 8 µl 4 NEBNext Second Strand Synthesis Enzyme Mix 4 µl 5 Nuclease-free Water 48 µl 6 Total Volume 80 µl Second Strand Synthesis Reaction Volume First Strand Synthesis Product (Step 40) 20 µl NEBNext Second Strand Synthesis Reaction Buffer with dUTP (10X) 8 µl NEBNext Second Strand Synthesis Enzyme Mix 4 µl Nuclease-free Water 48 µl Total Volume 80 µl NEBNext Second Strand Synthesis Reaction Buffer with dUTP Mix New England Biolabs Catalog #E7426 NEBNext Second Strand Synthesis Enzyme Mix New England Biolabs Catalog #E7425 Nuclease-free Water New England Biolabs Catalog #E7764

steps.title: Keeping the tube on ice, mix...
steps.instruction: Keeping the tube on ice, mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down at least 10 times.

steps.title: Incubate in a thermocycler for 01:00:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate in a thermocycler for 01:00:00 at 16 °C with the heated lid set at 40 °C (or off).

steps.title: Vortex SPRIselect Beads or NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex SPRIselect Beads or NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend. NEBNext Sample Purification Beads New England Biolabs Catalog #E7767

steps.title: Add 144 µL (1
steps.instruction: Add 144 µL (1.8X) of resuspended beads to the second strand synthesis reaction (~ 80 µL ). Mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Briefly spin the tube in a...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge to collect any sample on the sides of the tube. Place the tube on a magnet to separate beads from the supernatant. After the solution is clear, carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads, which contain DNA. Caution: Do not discard beads.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to the tube while in the magnetic rack. Incubate at room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Fresh 80% Ethanol Contributed by users

steps.title: Repeat Step 54 once for a...
steps.instruction: Repeat Step 54 once for a total of 2 washing steps. go to step #54

steps.title: Air dry the beads for up...
steps.instruction: Air dry the beads for up to 5 minutes while the tube is on the magnetic rack with lid open. Caution: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA target. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack, they are too dry.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack. Elute the DNA from the beads by adding 53 µL 0.1X TE Buffer (provided) to the beads. Mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. Quickly spin the tube and incubate for 00:02:00 at room temperature. Place the tube on the magnetic rack until the solution is clear. (0.1X) TE Buffer New England Biolabs Catalog #E7763

steps.title: Remove 50 µL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Remove 50 µL of the supernatant and transfer to a clean nuclease-free PCR tube. If you need to stop at this point in the protocol samples can be stored at –20°C.

steps.title: Assemble the end prep reaction on...
steps.instruction: Assemble the end prep reaction on ice by adding the following components to the second strand synthesis product from Step 58. %s %s End Prep Reaction Volume Second Strand Synthesis Product (Step 52) 50 µl NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer 7 µl NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix 3 µl Total Volume 60 µl If a master mix is made, add 10 µL of master mix to 50 µL of cDNA for the End Prep reaction. NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer New England Biolabs Catalog #E7647 NEBNext Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix New England Biolabs Catalog #E7646

steps.title: Set a 100 μl or 200...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 μl or 200 μl pipette to 50 μl and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube. It is important to mix well. The presence of a small amount of bubbles will not interfere with performance.

steps.title: Incubate the sample in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample in a thermocycler with the heated lid set at ≥ 75 °C as follows. 00:30:00 at 20 °C 00:30:00 at 65 °C Hold at 4 °C

steps.title: A B 1 Ligation Reaction Volume...
steps.instruction: A B 1 Ligation Reaction Volume 2 End Prepped DNA (
2.5 µl 4 NEBNext Ligation Enhancer 1 µl 5 NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix 30 µl 6 Total Volume 93.5 µl Ligation Reaction Volume End Prepped DNA (

2.5 µl NEBNext Ligation Enhancer 1 µl NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix 30 µl Total Volume 93.5 µl The Ligation Master Mix and Ligation Enhancer can be mixed ahead of time and is stable for at least 8 hours @ 4 °C . Do not premix the Ligation Master Mix, Ligation Enhancer and adaptor prior to use in the Adaptor Ligation Step. NEBNext Ligation Enhancer New England Biolabs Catalog #E7374 NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix New England Biolabs Catalog #E7648

steps.title: Set a 100 μl or 200...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 μl or 200 μl pipette to 80 μl and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube. [ ! ] Caution: The NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix is very viscous. Care should be taken to ensure adequate mixing of the ligation reaction, as incomplete mixing will result in reduced ligation efficiency. The presence of a small amount of bubbles will not interfere with performance.

steps.title: Incubate 15 minutes at 20°C in...
steps.instruction: Incubate 15 minutes at 20°C in a thermocycler with the heated lid off. 00:15:00, Incubate

steps.title: Add 3 µL (blue or red)...
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL (blue or red) USER™ Enzyme to the ligation mixture from Step 66, resulting in total volume of 96.5 µL NEBNext USER Enzyme New England Biolabs Catalog #E7458

steps.title: Mix well and incubate at 37...
steps.instruction: Mix well and incubate at 37 °C for 00:15:00 with the heated lid set to ≥ 45 °C .

steps.title: [ ! ] Note
steps.instruction: [ ! ] Note: If you are selecting for libraries with larger insert size (> 200 nt) follow the size selection recommendations in the Appendix ( manual ). Add 87 µL (0.9X) resuspended SPRIselect Beads or NEBNext Sample Purification Beads and mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times. NEBNext Sample Purification Beads New England Biolabs Catalog #E7767

steps.title: Incubate for 00:10:00 at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:10:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Quickly spin the tube in a...
steps.instruction: Quickly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge and place the tube on an appropriate magnetic rack to separate beads from the supernatant. After the solution is clear (~ 00:05:00 ), discard the supernatant that contains unwanted fragments. Caution: Do not discard beads.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to the tube while in the magnetic rack. Incubate at room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared) Contributed by users

steps.title: Repeat Step 73 once for a...
steps.instruction: Repeat Step 73 once for a total of 2 washing steps. go to step #73

steps.title: Briefly spin the tube, and put...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin the tube, and put the tube back in the magnetic rack.

steps.title: Completely remove the residual ethanol, and...
steps.instruction: Completely remove the residual ethanol, and air dry beads until the beads are dry for up to 5 minutes while the tube is on the magnetic rack with the lid open. [ ! ] Caution: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA target. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack, they are too dry.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack. Elute DNA target from the beads by adding 17 µL 0.1X TE (provided) to the beads. Mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down. Quickly spin the tube and incubate for 00:02:00 at room temperature. Put the tube in the magnet until the solution is clear.

steps.title: Without disturbing the bead pellet, transfer...
steps.instruction: Without disturbing the bead pellet, transfer 15 µL of the supernatant to a clean PCR tube and proceed to PCR enrichment. If you need to stop at this point in the protocol samples can be stored at –20°C.

[ ! ] Use Option A for any NEBNext oligos kit where index primers are supplied in tubes. These kits have the forward and reverse primers supplied in separate tubes. Primers are supplied at 10 μM each. Use Option B for any NEBNext oligos kit where index primers are supplied in a 96-well plate format. These kits have the forward and reverse (i7 and i5) primers combined. Primers are supplied at 10 μM combined (5 μM each).

steps.title: Set up the PCR reaction as...
steps.instruction: Set up the PCR reaction as described below based on the type of oligos (PCR primers) used. Option A: Forward and Reverse Primers Separate: %s %s Component Volume Per One Library Adaptor Ligated DNA (Step 72) 14 µl NEBNext Ultra II Q5 Master Mix 25 µl Universal PCR Primer/i5 Primer*,** 5 µl Index (X) Primer/i7 Primer*,** 5 µl Total Volume 50 µl Option B: Forward and Reverse Primers Combined: %s %s Component Volume Per One Library Adaptor ligated DNA (Step 72) 15 µl NEBNext Ultra II Q5 Master Mix 25 µl Index (X)/Universal Primer Mix* 10 µl Total Volume 50 µl * NEBNext Oligos must be purchased separately from the library prep kit. Refer to the corresponding NEBNext Oligo kit manual for determining valid barcode combinations. ** Use only one i7 primer/ index primer per sample. Use only one i5 primer (or the universal primer for single index kits) per sample

steps.title: Mix well by gently pipetting up and down 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix well by gently pipetting up and down 10 times. Quickly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge.

steps.title: Place the tube on a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a thermocycler with the heated lid set to 105 °C and perform PCR amplification using the following PCR cycling conditions (refer to Table 82.A and Table 82.B): Table 82.A: A B C D 1 Cycle Step Temp Time Cycles 2 Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 seconds 1 3 Denaturation 98°C 10 seconds 8–16*,** 4 Annealing/Extension 65°C 75 seconds 5 Final Extension 65°C 5 minutes 1 6 Hold 4°C ∞   Cycle Step Temp Time Cycles Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 seconds 1 Denaturation 98°C 10 seconds 7–16*,** Annealing/Extension 65°C 75 seconds Final Extension 65°C 5 minutes 1 Hold 4°C ∞ * The number of PCR cycles should be adjusted based on RNA input. ** It is important to limit the number of PCR cycles to avoid overamplification. If overamplification occurs, a second peak ~ 1,000 bp will appear on the Bioanalyzer trace (See Figure 7.2 in manual ). Table 82.B: Recommended PCR cycles based on total RNA input amount: %s %s Total RNA Input Recommended PCR Cycles 1,000 ng 7–8 100 ng 11–12 10 ng Note: PCR cycles are recommended based on high quality Universal Human Reference Total RNA. It may require optimization based on the sample quality to prevent PCR over-amplification.

steps.title: Vortex SPRIselect Beads or NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend
steps.instruction: Vortex SPRIselect Beads or NEBNext Sample Purification Beads to resuspend.

steps.title: Add 45 µL (0
steps.instruction: Add 45 µL (0.9X) of resuspended beads to the PCR reaction (~ 50 µL ). Mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down at least 10 times.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Quickly spin the tube in a...
steps.instruction: Quickly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge and place the tube on an appropriate magnetic rack to separate beads from the supernatant. After the solution is clear (~ 00:05:00 ), carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Be careful not to disturb the beads that contain DNA targets. Caution: Do not discard beads.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to the tube while in the magnetic rack. Incubate at room temperature for 00:00:30 , and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant. 80% Ethanol (freshly prepared) Contributed by users

steps.title: Repeat go to step #87 once...
steps.instruction: Repeat go to step #87 once for a total of 2 washing steps.

steps.title: Air dry the beads for up...
steps.instruction: Air dry the beads for up to 5 minutes while the tube is on the magnetic rack with the lid open. Caution: Do not over-dry the beads. This may result in lower recovery of DNA target. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack, they are too dry.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack. Elute the DNA target from the beads by adding 23 µL 0.1X TE (provided) to the beads. Mix well on a vortex mixer or by pipetting up and down ten times. Quickly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge and incubate for 00:02:00 at room temperature. Place the tube in the magnetic rack until the solution is clear.

steps.title: Transfer 20 µL of the supernatant...
steps.instruction: Transfer 20 µL of the supernatant to a clean PCR tube, and store at –20°C.

steps.title: Run 1 µL library on a DNA 1000 chip
steps.instruction: Run 1 µL library on a DNA 1000 chip. If the library yield is too low to quantify on this chip, please run the samples on a DNA High Sensitivity chip. A dilution may be necessary for running on a Bioanalyzer High Sensitivity DNA Chip.

steps.title: Check that the electropherogram shows a...
steps.instruction: Check that the electropherogram shows a narrow distribution with a peak size approximately 300 bp. Note: If a peak at ~ 80 bp (primers) or 128 bp (adaptor-dimer) is visible in the Bioanalyzer traces, bring up the sample volume (from Step 91) to 50 μl with 0.1X TE buffer and repeat the SPRIselect Bead or NEBNext Sample Purification Bead Cleanup Step (Section "Purification of the PCR Reaction using SPRIselect Beads or NEBNext Sample Purification Beads").

steps.title: Figure 94
steps.instruction: Figure 94: Example of RNA library size distribution on a Bioanalyzer.
